title: SuperBEST v3 Cost Analysis — Biology Block 3: Enzyme Kinetics
key_observation: All six basic enzyme-kinetics equations are RATIONAL functions — no exp or ln appear anywhere. Primitives used are: add, mul, recip (implementing division as mul·recip). Division a/b is computed as mul(a, recip(b)), costing 2 (recip) + 2 (mul) = 4 nodes.

division_decomposition.note: SuperBEST has no 'div' primitive. Division a/b = mul(a, recip(b)).
division_decomposition.cost: recip(b)=2 + mul(a, recip(b))=2 → total 4 nodes per division

equations.name: Michaelis-Menten
equations.notes: Canonical rational function. The denominator add costs 3; each mul costs 2; recip for division costs 2. Result: 9 nodes.

equations.name: Lineweaver-Burk (double reciprocal)
equations.notes: Two reciprocals needed ([S] and Vmax). Vmax reciprocal is shared across both terms. Add at the end costs 3. Total 11 nodes.
equations.optimization_note: If recip(Vmax) is computed once and reused (DAG sharing), the logical cost stays 11 but tree size is 11; with DAG the shared node is counted once.

equations.name: Competitive inhibition
equations.breakdown: recip(2) + mul(2) + add(3) + mul(2) + add(3) + mul(2) + recip(2) + mul(2) = 18
equations.notes: The inhibition factor α = 1 + [I]/Ki adds a recip+mul+add sub-tree (7 nodes) on top of base Michaelis-Menten. '1' can be a free terminal (cost 0).

equations.name: Uncompetitive inhibition
equations.breakdown: recip(2) + mul(2) + add(3) + mul(2) + add(3) + mul(2) + recip(2) + mul(2) = 18
equations.notes: Structurally isomorphic to competitive inhibition — same cost 18. The only difference is whether α multiplies Km (competitive) or [S] (uncompetitive); the operator count is identical.

equations.name: Mixed inhibition (general)
equations.formula: v = Vmax·[S] / (α·Km + α'·[S])   where α = 1+[I]/Ki, α' = 1+[I]/Ki'
equations.breakdown: alpha: recip(2)+mul(2)+add(3)=7 | alpha_prime: recip(2)+mul(2)+add(3)=7 | denom: mul(2)+mul(2)+add(3)=7 | division: mul(2)+recip(2)+mul(2)=6 | total=27
equations.notes: [I] appears in both α and α' sub-trees but is a shared terminal (cost 0 each time in a DAG). '1' is a free terminal shared across both add(1,…) steps. Two separate Ki constants (Ki and Ki') each need their own recip.

equations.name: Enzyme turnover
equations.notes: Minimal rational expression. If [E]total is absorbed into Vmax as a pre-scaled constant (i.e., kcat is itself a free terminal), cost = 0. Otherwise 4 nodes.

equations.name: Specificity constant efficiency

alternatives.if_both_terminals: 0 operator nodes — ratio is itself a free parameter
alternatives.if_kcat_from_eq6: kcat already costs 4 nodes; then add recip(Km)+mul = 4 more → 8 total from raw Vmax/[E]total/Km

equations.notes: If kcat and Km are both free terminals (parameters known from fitting), cost is 4. If kcat must be derived from Vmax/[E]total first, total cost from raw inputs is 8.

cross_cutting_findings.no_exp_or_ln: None of the seven equations use exp or ln. All are rational functions composed entirely of polynomial numerators and denominators.
cross_cutting_findings.primitive_add_needed_when: Any sum appears in the denominator or as part of an inhibition factor. Pure-division expressions (eq6, eq7) need no add.
cross_cutting_findings.division_cost: Each division adds 4 nodes (recip=2 + mul=2). This is the dominant structural cost driver.
cross_cutting_findings.inhibition_factor_cost: Each inhibition factor α = 1+[I]/Ki costs 7 nodes (recip+mul+add). Mixed inhibition has two such factors, explaining its high cost of 27.
cross_cutting_findings.michaelis_menten_is_minimal_rational: v = Vmax·[S]/(Km+[S]) achieves enzyme rate as a rational function with just 9 nodes — the minimum for a biologically meaningful saturating curve.
cross_cutting_findings.lineweaver_burk_inversion_penalty: Taking double reciprocals adds 2 extra recip nodes (one for 1/v implicit, one for 1/[S]) and reorganizes the tree, increasing cost from 9 to 11 — a small penalty for linearization.
cross_cutting_findings.shared_terminals_are_free: Parameters like [I] or '1' appearing multiple times in the tree are free terminals each time — no additional node cost for reuse.
